title: Effect of complex training on lower limb strength and running economy in adolescent distance runners
training_status: At least 3 years systematic training; at least 35 km/week
notes: Heavy resistance preceded matched plyometrics with 3-4 min recovery; endurance placement was not reported; no universal sequence inference.

title: Effects of Strength Training on Postpubertal Adolescent Distance Runners
population: 25 postpubertal adolescent distance runners enrolled; 18 completed
notes: Twice-weekly plyometric sprint and resistance work supported economy and strength signals; sequence and running separation were not reported in the abstract.

title: Concurrent endurance and explosive type strength training improves neuromuscular and anaerobic characteristics in young distance runners
notes: Nineteen percent of endurance training was replaced by explosive work; speed improved while VO2max and economy were unchanged; scheduling was not reported.

title: A preliminary investigation into concurrent aerobic and resistance training in youth runners
population: 12 competitive youth runners pair-matched by maturity and 3 km performance
notes: Tiny null/adverse signal; resistance frequency conflicts between abstract and review table; order and separation remain unresolved.

title: Efficacy of depth jumps to elicit a post-activation performance enhancement in junior endurance runners
population: 17 national or international male junior middle-distance runners
training_status: National or international junior runners; VO2max 70.7 +/- 5.2
notes: Six depth jumps followed by 10 min passive recovery improved acute running economy but not time to exhaustion; chronic scheduling transfer is unsupported.

title: The Effects of Concurrent Strength and Endurance Training on Physical Fitness and Athletic Performance in Youth: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
population: 518 youth across 15 studies; 11 youth endurance-athlete studies
notes: Small athletic-performance effect but null cardiorespiratory and economy effects; sequence and separation were not meta-analyzed.

title: Effects of complex training versus heavy resistance training on neuromuscular adaptation, running economy and 5-km performance in well-trained distance runners
notes: Morning endurance and afternoon strength plus heavy-to-plyometric complex pairs improved several outcomes; AM-PM hours and reverse order were not tested.

title: Effects of plyometric training on endurance and explosive strength performance in competitive middle- and long-distance runners
population: 36 highly competitive middle- and long-distance runners
notes: Six weeks of added plyometrics improved 2.4 km sprint and jump outcomes; shared-day placement was not verified.

title: Effect of concurrent strength and endurance training on run performance and biomechanics in recreational runners
population: 30 moderately trained distance runners inexperienced in strength training
notes: Concurrent training improved 2 km time and time to exhaustion; strength dose sequence and separation were unavailable in the abstract.

title: Concurrent speed endurance and resistance training improves performance, running economy, and muscle NHE1 in moderately trained runners
notes: Speed endurance preceded heavy resistance in the same session while running volume was reduced; outcomes improved but reverse order was not tested.

title: Effects of different uphill interval-training programs on running economy and performance
population: 20 well-trained runners randomized across five uphill programs
notes: Hill component evidence showed no clearly superior program for 5 km performance and did not test placement relative to strength or plyometrics.

title: Physiological Response Differences between Run and Cycle High Intensity Interval Training Program in Recreational Middle Age Female Runners
training_status: At least two habitual runs per week
notes: Small run-versus-cycle HIIT trial; habitual-training placement was not standardized and cycling cannot be inferred to be equivalent or lower fatigue.

title: Cross-training between running and cycling: effects on VO2max and running performance - a systematic review and meta-analysis
population: Seven heterogeneous randomized trials from untrained through competitive adults
notes: Pooled VO2max and running-performance effects were null with wide intervals; modality equivalence recovery benefit and placement are unsupported.

title: The Effect of Strength Training Methods on Middle-Distance and Long-Distance Runners' Athletic Performance: A Systematic Review With Meta-analysis
population: 894 middle- and long-distance runners across 38 studies
notes: High-load and combined strength methods improved running performance but endurance-strength order and separation were not meta-analyzed.

title: Compatibility of Concurrent Aerobic and Strength Training for Skeletal Muscle Size and Function: An Updated Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
notes: Maximal strength and hypertrophy were null overall while explosive strength showed a small same-session interference signal; adult subgroup bins are not youth thresholds.

title: The Role of Intra-session Exercise Sequence in the Interference Effect: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis
notes: Resistance-before-endurance favored dynamic strength only; aerobic hypertrophy and static strength outcomes were null and do not support a universal order.

title: Adaptations to High-Intensity Interval Training and Strength Training Performed in Sequence: A Randomized Controlled Trial
notes: Cycling HIIT and resistance were separated about 3 h; broad adaptation was similar but explosive outcomes varied by order.

title: Specific training effects of concurrent aerobic and strength exercises depend on recovery duration
notes: Resistance always preceded aerobic work with 0 h 6 h or 24 h separation; one indirect team-sport trial cannot define a youth threshold.

title: The order of exercise during concurrent training for rehabilitation does not alter acute genetic expression, mitochondrial enzyme activity or improvements in muscle function
population: 18 healthy inactive adults after two weeks unilateral knee immobilization
notes: Immediate endurance-resistance order did not alter most adaptations; rehabilitation and detraining make transfer to trained youth runners very low.

title: Concurrent training: a meta-analysis examining interference of aerobic and resistance exercises
notes: Historical interference synthesis with modality frequency and duration moderators; retained below the updated review and not usable as a youth calendar rule.

title: Development of Maximal Dynamic Strength During Concurrent Resistance and Endurance Training in Untrained, Moderately Trained, and Trained Individuals: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
notes: Trained adults showed lower strength development in same-session concurrent studies; study-defined under-20-min and over-2-h bins are not validated thresholds.
